title: Mantisalca salmantica
intro: Mantisalca salmantica, also known as Rock Rose, is a perennial plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has identified several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rock Rose include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Rock Rose is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
